session.assistant: The YAML in this comment is an older version that doesn't have node 1.7. The comment's YAML only goes up to 1.3, then jumps to 2.1. This is a stale copy — the authoritative YAML is in the issue body (which I already updated). Let me just push the prose table update.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Update objective comment with skipped status for node 1.7

session.tool_result:
{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dagster-io/erk/issues/comments/4011633841","html_url":"https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/issues/8832#issuecomment-4011633841","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dagster-io/erk/issues/8832","id":4011633841,"node_id":"IC_kwDOPxC3hc7vHKyx","user":{"login":"schrockn","id":28738937,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4NzM4OTM3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28738937?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn","html_url":"https://github.com/schrockn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/schrockn/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2026-03-06T13:03:42Z","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:56:06Z","body":"
Objective # Objective: Decouple CLI Commands from Git Repo Requirement Many erk CLI commands require being in a git repository but only use it to identify the GitHub owner/repo for API calls. By adding an optional `--repo owner/repo` flag, these commands can run from anywhere with just `gh` authentication. ## Goal Any erk command that primarily interacts with the GitHub API can run from any directory when given `--repo owner/repo`. Commands that genuinely need local git state (worktree management, implementation, landing) continue to require a repo. The end state: ```bash # From any directory, no git repo needed: cd /tmp erk one-shot --repo schrockn/erk "fix the typo in README" erk pr list --repo schrockn/erk erk objective list --repo schrockn/erk erk pr dispatch --repo schrockn/erk 123 ``` When inside a git repo, `--repo` is optional — owner/repo is auto-discovered from git remote as today. When outside a git repo, `--repo` is required. Error message when missing: "Not in a git repository. Use --repo owner/repo to specify." ## Design Decisions - **`-R`/`--repo` as optional flag per command** (not global): Only commands that support remote operation get the flag. Matches `gh` CLI convention. When in a git repo without `--repo`, behavior is unchanged. - **HttpClient for remote operations**: Use the existing `HttpClient` (httpx-based) for all GitHub API calls in remote mode, bypassing the `gh api` subprocess calls that require git context for `{owner}/{repo}` template resolution. - **`remote_github/` gateway package**: New 3-file gateway package (`abc.py`, `real.py`, `fake.py`) with HttpClient-based GitHub operations that take explicit `owner`/`repo` params instead of `repo_root: Path`. Shared across all converted commands. - **In-repo behavior unchanged**: When running inside a git repo without `--repo`, all existing code paths are preserved. Zero behavior change for current users. ## Roadmap ### Phase 1: Steelthread: one-shot outside git repos (6 PR) | Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|----| | 1.1 | Always create HttpClient in context (remove git-repo guard) | done | #8835 | | 1.2 | Create RemoteGitHub gateway package (abc/real/fake) with HttpClient-based GitHub operations that take explicit owner/repo params instead of repo_root Path | done | #8835 | | 1.3 | Add --repo flag to one-shot command with remote dispatch path | done | #8835 | | 1.4 | Create @no_repo_required decorator and sentinel gateway infrastructure. Add NoRepoGit/NoRepoGitHub sentinel implementations (following GraphiteDisabled pattern) that raise descriptive errors when repo-dependent operations are called outside a repo context. Wire sentinels into context construction when isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel). The decorator marks Click commands as not requiring a git repo and is inspectable via command.no_repo_required attribute. All commands converted in phases 2 and 3 must use @no_repo_required and route through RemoteGitHub instead of the local subprocess-based GitHub gateway. | in-progress | #8880 | | 1.5 | Make RemoteGitHub the canonical one-shot dispatch path: eliminate the parallel local git dispatch code (one_shot_dispatch.py dispatch_one_shot), unify onto the REST API path (one_shot_remote_dispatch.py), delete the old local dispatch module. Apply @no_repo_required to one-shot command. When --repo is not passed, extract owner/repo from ctx.repo.github and still use RemoteGitHub. | in-progress | #8883 | | 1.6 | Rename GitHub/RealGitHub ABC to LocalGitHub/RealLocalGitHub to clarify it is the subprocess-based local-repo gateway (vs RemoteGitHub REST API gateway). Mechanical bulk rename across ~50 files. | in-progress | #8888 | | 1.7 | ~~Rename GitHubIssues/RealGitHubIssues/FakeGitHubIssues/DryRunGitHubIssues to LocalGitHubIssues/RealLocalGitHubIssues/FakeLocalGitHubIssues/DryRunLocalGitHubIssues. Same mechanical LibCST bulk rename as 1.6, applied to the issues sub-gateway (~142 files).~~ | skipped | - | ### Phase 2: Read-only plan and objective commands (0 PR) | Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|----| | 2.1 | Convert pr read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: pr list/dash, pr view, pr check, pr log, pr close, pr duplicate-check. Each command routes through RemoteGitHub when --repo is provided or when no local repo is present. | pending | - | | 2.2 | Convert objective read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: objective list, objective view, objective check, objective close. Same pattern as 2.1. | pending | - | | 2.3 | Refactor discover_repo_context to accept explicit owner/repo as alternative to git discovery, enabling commands to resolve repo identity from --repo flag without a local clone | pending | - | ### Phase 3: Remaining dispatch commands (0 PR) | Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|----| | 3.1 | Convert pr create to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | | 3.2 | Convert pr dispatch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | | 3.3 | Convert launch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | | 3.4 | Convert objective plan --one-shot to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | **Test:** cd /tmp && erk pr create --repo owner/repo -f plan.md creates plan. erk pr dispatch --repo owner/repo 123 dispatches.
objective-roadmap ```yaml schema_version: '4' nodes: - id: '1.1' slug: http-client-always description: Always create HttpClient in context (remove git-repo guard) status: pending pr: null - id: '1.2' slug: remote-github-module description: Create remote_github.py module with HttpClient-based GitHub operations status: pending pr: null - id: '1.3' slug: one-shot-repo-flag description: Add --repo flag to one-shot command with remote dispatch path status: pending pr: null - id: '2.1' slug: pr-read-commands description: Add --repo flag to pr list/dash, pr view, pr check, pr log, pr close, pr duplicate-check status: pending pr: null - id: '2.2' slug: objective-read-commands description: Add --repo flag to objective list, objective view, objective check, objective close status: pending pr: null - id: '2.3' slug: discover-repo-refactor description: Refactor discover_repo_context to accept explicit owner/repo as alternative to git discovery status: pending pr: null - id: '3.1' slug: pr-create-remote description: Add --repo flag to pr create with remote dispatch path status: pending pr: null - id: '3.2' slug: pr-dispatch-remote description: Add --repo flag to pr dispatch with remote dispatch path status: pending pr: null - id: '3.3' slug: launch-remote description: Add --repo flag to launch with remote dispatch path status: pending pr: null - id: '3.4' slug: objective-plan-remote description: Add --repo flag to objective plan --one-shot with remote dispatch path status: pending pr: null ```
## Exploration Notes **Current architecture**: `create_context()` in `src/erk/core/context.py` creates `RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel` via `discover_repo_or_sentinel()`. Commands check for `NoRepoSentinel` or call `discover_repo_context()` which raises `FileNotFoundError`. The `HttpClient` was previously only created when in a repo — PR #8835 removed that guard, so `HttpClient` is now always created when a GitHub token is available. **Key bottleneck**: The GitHub gateway (`RealGitHub`) uses `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/...` commands that require git context for template resolution. The `HttpClient` already exists and can make direct REST API calls with explicit URLs -- no git context needed. **One-shot workflow insight**: The CI workflow (`one-shot.yml`) already writes the prompt from the workflow input if `.erk/impl-context/prompt.md` isn't on the branch (lines 110-116). So local git plumbing (branch create, commit prompt, push) can be replaced entirely with GitHub API calls. **Commands NOT in scope** (genuinely need local git): `wt *`, `pr checkout`, `pr teleport`, `stack move`, `implement`, `land`, `status`, `init`, `config set` (repo-specific settings). ## Related Documentation - Skills to load: `dignified-python`, `fake-driven-testing` - Key files: `src/erk/core/context.py`, `src/erk/core/repo_discovery.py`, `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py`, `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/http/abc.py`

1→# Updating an Objective 2→ 3→See [format.md](format.md) for: 4→ 5→- **Action Comment Template** - What to include in update comments 6→- **When to Update** - Triggers for updating vs waiting 7→- **Update Examples** - Real examples of the two-step workflow 8→ 9→## Quick Summary 10→ 11→The two-step pattern applies to ALL objective changes, not just completions: 12→ 13→| Change Type | Comment | Body Update | 14→| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | 15→| Complete a node | "Action: Completed X" | Status → done, add PR | 16→| Add context | "Action: Added X" | Add section to body | 17→| Refine decision | "Action: Refined X" | Update decision text | 18→| Add phase | "Action: Added Phase X" | Add phase to roadmap | 19→| Hit blocker | "Action: Identified blocker" | Status → blocked | 20→| Reconcile after PR | "Action: [Title]" with Body Reconciliation subsection | Update stale sections | 21→ 22→**Why both steps?** 23→ 24→- Comment = changelog (when/why things changed) 25→- Body = source of truth (current complete state) 26→ 27→Comment first (captures the moment), then body (reflects new state). 28→

1→# Objective Format Reference 2→ 3→Complete templates and examples for objective issues. 4→ 5→## Contents 6→ 7→- [Issue Body Template](#issue-body-template) 8→- [Action Comment Template](#action-comment-template) 9→ - [When to Update](#when-to-update) 10→ - [What to Update in Issue Body](#what-to-update-in-issue-body) 11→- [Example: Steelthread-Structured Objective](#example-steelthread-structured-objective) 12→- [Common Patterns](#common-patterns) 13→- [Update Examples](#update-examples) 14→- [Programmatic Roadmap Operations](#programmatic-roadmap-operations) 15→ 16→## Issue Body Template 17→ 18→The issue body represents the **current state** of the objective. Update it as progress is made. 19→ 20→```markdown 21→# Objective: [Title] 22→ 23→> [1-2 sentence summary of what this objective achieves] 24→ 25→## Goal 26→ 27→[What success looks like - concrete end state. Be specific about deliverables.] 28→ 29→## Design Decisions 30→

session.assistant: The skill docs cover the two-step pattern (comment + body update) and mention updating roadmap tables, but they don't explicitly say "re-render the prose roadmap table in the objective comment when you update the YAML roadmap in the issue body." The `erk exec update-objective-node` command at line 575 might handle this — let me check.
session.tool_result:
Usage: erk exec update-objective-node [OPTIONS] ISSUE_NUMBER
Update node PR cells in an objective's roadmap table.
Options: --node TEXT Node ID(s) to update (e.g., '1.3') [required] --pr TEXT PR reference (e.g., '#456', or '' to clear). Omit to preserve existing. --status [pending|planning|done|in_progress|blocked|skipped] Explicit status to set (default: infer from PR value) --include-body Include the fully-mutated issue body in JSON output as 'updated_body' -h, --help Show this message and exit.
session.assistant: So there IS a programmatic tool (`erk exec update-objective-node`) that handles this — I should have used it instead of manually editing the YAML. Let me check if it updates both the issue body YAML and the comment's prose table.
session.tool_result:

When --repo is not passed, extract owner/repo from ctx.repo.github and still use RemoteGitHub.' status: in_progress pr: '#8883' - id: '1.6' slug: rename-github-to-local description: Rename GitHub/RealGitHub ABC to LocalGitHub/RealLocalGitHub to clarify it is the subprocess-based local-repo gateway (vs RemoteGitHub REST API gateway). Each command routes through RemoteGitHub when --repo is provided or when no local repo is present.' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.2' slug: objective-read-commands description: 'Convert objective read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: objective list, objective view, objective check, objective close. Same pattern as 2.1.' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.3' slug: discover-repo-refactor description: Refactor discover_repo_context to accept explicit owner/repo as alternative to git discovery, enabling commands to resolve repo identity from --repo flag without a local clone status: pending pr: null - id: '3.1' slug: pr-create-remote description: Convert pr create to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub status: pending pr: null - id: '3.2' slug: pr-dispatch-remote description: Convert pr dispatch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub status: pending pr: null - id: '3.3' slug: launch-remote description: Convert launch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub status: pending pr: null - id: '3.4' slug: objective-plan-remote description: Convert objective plan --one-shot to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub status: pending pr: null ```

session.assistant:
OK so `erk exec update-objective-node` updates the **issue body YAML** but does NOT update the **objective comment's prose roadmap table**. That's the gap — the prose table in the comment still needs a separate update.
To answer your question: **No, the objective skill docs don't explicitly instruct agents to re-render the prose roadmap table in the objective comment when mutating node status.** The docs mention updating "roadmap tables" but don't call out the two-location problem (issue body YAML vs. comment prose table).
I should add this to the skill. The rule would be:
> When mutating objective node status, use `erk exec update-objective-node` for the issue body YAML, then also update the prose roadmap table in the objective comment (identified by `objective_comment_id` in the header metadata). Both must stay in sync.
Want me to update the objective skill docs (likely `updating.md` or `format.md`) with this requirement?
session.user: Yes make a plan to do that. There shoudl also be an erk objective check that validates that the roadmap and the header are in sync. This should be called in any flow that muates the objecitve

tool_use.name: Agent

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 810 chars]... check afterward? - Is there already any mention of syncing the prose table with the YAML?

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding of the objective skill documentation structure. Let me create a summary document for the user.
## Summary: Objective Skill Documentation Structure and Roadmap Table Re-Rendering
Based on my exploration of the objective skill documentation, here's what I found:
### Current Documentation Structure
**Main Objective Skill** (`.claude/skills/objective/SKILL.md`): - Overview of objectives vs erk-plans - 12 key design principles - Quick reference for common operations - References to 4 detailed reference files
**Reference Files** (`.claude/skills/objective/references/`): 1. **workflow.md** - Creating objectives, spawning plans, resuming work, steelthread structuring 2. **updating.md** - Two-step update pattern (comment first, body second) 3. **format.md** - Complete templates, examples, action comment format, update patterns 4. **closing.md** - Proactive closing triggers and procedures
### Key Learned Documentation
Located in `docs/learned/objectives/`: - **roadmap-format-versioning.md** - Current 4-column canonical format and versioning strategy - **roadmap-mutation-patterns.md** - Surgical vs full-body update patterns, when to use each - **roadmap-mutation-semantics.md** - Write-time status computation vs parse-time inference - **objective-roadmap-check.md** - Semantic validation beyond structural parsing - **objective-create-workflow.md** - 3-layer pipeline for objective creation - **roadmap-parser-api.md** - Shared parser functions and their consumers
... [75 lines omitted] ...

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report:
## Summary
Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase and documentation, here are the answers to your four questions:
### 1. The `erk objective check` command
**File Path:** `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/src/erk/cli/commands/objective/check_cmd.py`
**Function:** `validate_objective()` (lines 71-218) and CLI command `check_objective()` (lines 221-249)
**Current Validations (7 checks):** 1. **erk-objective label** — Issue must have the label (line 101) 2. **Roadmap parses successfully** — Structural validation via `parse_roadmap()` (line 108) 3. **Status/PR consistency** — Steps with PR references must have status in `[in_progress, done, planning, skipped]` (lines 148-163) 4. **No orphaned done statuses** — `done` steps must have a PR reference (lines 165-174) 5. **Sequential phase numbering** — Phase keys must be strictly ordered (lines 176-183) 6. **v2 format integrity** — `objective-header` must have `objective_comment_id` (lines 185-192) 7. **PR reference format** — PR cells must use `#` prefix like `#123` (lines 194-203)
The command supports both human-readable `[PASS]/[FAIL]` output (default) and JSON output via `--json-output` flag.
---
### 2. The `erk exec update-objective-node` script
**File Path:** `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py`
**What it does:**
... [81 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Good findings. The exec scripts already re-render the comment table — my mistake was manually editing the YAML instead of using `erk exec update-objective-node`. But there's no validation check to catch when they drift. Let me read the key implementation files.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Validate an objective's format and roadmap consistency.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→from pathlib import Path 6→ 7→import click 8→ 9→from erk.cli.alias import alias 10→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 11→from erk.cli.github_parsing import parse_issue_identifier 12→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, RepoContext 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 16→ find_metadata_block, 17→ has_metadata_block, 18→) 19→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.dependency_graph import ( 20→ DependencyGraph, 21→ build_graph, 22→ compute_graph_summary, 23→ find_graph_next_node, 24→ phases_from_graph, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import ( 27→ parse_roadmap, 28→ serialize_phases, 29→) 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys
... [292 lines omitted] ...
38→ """Validation completed (may have passed or failed checks). 43→ failed_count: Number of failed checks 44→ graph: Dependency graph of roadmap nodes (empty if parsing failed) 47→ validation_errors: Parser-level warnings from roadmap parsing 53→ failed_count: int 57→ validation_errors: list[str] 62→class ObjectiveValidationError: 65→ error: str 68→ObjectiveValidationResult = ObjectiveValidationSuccess | ObjectiveValidationError 91→ ObjectiveValidationError if unable to complete validation 98→ return ObjectiveValidationError(error=f"Issue #{issue_number} not found") 108→ phases, validation_errors = parse_roadmap(issue.body) 115→ failed_count = sum(1 for passed, _ in checks if not passed) 117→ passed=failed_count == 0, 119→ failed_count=failed_count, 123→ validation_errors=[], 130→ # Block exists but failed to parse 131→ checks.append((False, f"Roadmap parses successfully ({'; '.join(validation_errors)})")) 132→ failed_count = sum(1 for passed, _ in checks if not passed) 136→ failed_count=failed_count, 140→ validation_errors=validation_errors, 207→ failed_count = sum(1 for passed, _ in checks if not passed) 210→ passed=failed_count == 0, 212→ failed_count=failed_count, 216→ validation_errors=validation_errors, 254→ if isinstance(result, ObjectiveValidationError): 259→ "error": result.error, 278→ "validation_errors": result.validation_errors, 289→ if isinstance(result, ObjectiveValidationError): 291→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Failed to validate objective: {result.error}" 315→ check_word = "checks" if result.failed_count > 1 else "check" 318→ f"Objective validation failed ({result.failed_count} {check_word} failed)", fg="red"
session.assistant: Now let me read the roadmap module to understand `rerender_comment_roadmap` and the table parsing:
session.tool_result:
700→ end_idx = text.find(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END, start_idx) 701→ if end_idx == -1: 702→ return None 703→ 704→ content_start = start_idx + len(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START) 705→ section = text[content_start:end_idx] 706→ return (section, start_idx, end_idx + len(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END)) 707→ 708→ 709→def rerender_comment_roadmap(issue_body: str, comment_body: str) -> str | None: 710→ """Deterministically re-render the roadmap tables in a comment from YAML source of truth. 711→ 712→ Parses the roadmap YAML from the issue body, enriches phase names from the 713→ comment's markdown headers, renders new tables, and splices them into the 714→ comment's marker-bounded section. 715→ 716→ This replaces all per-node regex patching (``_replace_table_in_text``). 717→ 718→ Args: 719→ issue_body: The objective issue body containing the ``objective-roadmap`` 720→ metadata block (YAML source of truth). 721→ comment_body: The objective-body comment containing the rendered 722→ markdown tables bounded by ``<!-- erk:roadmap-table -->`` markers. 723→ 724→ Returns: 725→ Updated comment body with re-rendered tables, or None if no roadmap 726→ block or no markers found. 727→ """ 728→ raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(issue_body) 729→ matching_blocks = [block for block in raw_blocks if block.key == BlockKeys.OBJECTIVE_ROADMAP]
... [27 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Now I have a thorough understanding. The key insight is that `validate_objective` currently only checks the **issue body** — it doesn't fetch or validate the objective comment at all. The new check needs to:
1. Fetch the comment using `objective_comment_id` from the header 2. Parse node statuses from the prose roadmap table in the comment 3. Compare them against the YAML source of truth in the issue body
Let me also check how the comment is fetched:
session.tool_result:
packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/abc.py:150: def get_comment_by_id(self, repo_root: Path, comment_id: int) -> str: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/dry_run.py:85: def get_comment_by_id(self, repo_root: Path, comment_id: int) -> str: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/dry_run.py:87: return self._wrapped.get_comment_by_id(repo_root, comment_id) packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/real.py:338: def get_comment_by_id(self, repo_root: Path, comment_id: int) -> str: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/fake.py:308: def get_comment_by_id(self, repo_root: Path, comment_id: int) -> str:
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 5, offset: 0]
session.assistant: Now let me check how to parse node statuses from the prose roadmap table — is there existing parsing code for that?
session.tool_result: 686:def extract_roadmap_table_section(text: str) -> tuple[str, int, int] | None: 742: marker_section = extract_roadmap_table_section(comment_body)
session.assistant:
Good — there's no existing parser for extracting node data FROM the prose table. But the simpler approach is: instead of parsing the prose table, just re-render it from YAML and compare. If they differ, they're out of sync.
Now I have enough to write the plan.

1→"""In-memory fake implementation of GitHub issues for testing.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import replace 4→from datetime import UTC, datetime 5→from pathlib import Path 6→ 7→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import ( 9→ CreateIssueResult, 10→ IssueComment, 11→ IssueInfo, 12→ IssueNotFound, 13→ PRReference, 14→) 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import BodyContent, BodyFile, BodyText 16→ 17→ 18→class FakeGitHubIssues(GitHubIssues): 19→ """In-memory fake implementation for testing. 20→ 21→ All state is provided via constructor using keyword arguments. 22→ """ 23→ 24→ def __init__( 25→ self, 26→ *, 27→ issues: dict[int, IssueInfo] | None = None, 28→ next_issue_number: int = 1, 29→ labels: set[str] | None = None, 30→ comments: dict[int, list[str]] | None = None,
... [20 lines omitted] ...
34→ add_reaction_error: str | None = None, 35→ get_comments_error: str | None = None, 50→ add_reaction_error: If set, add_reaction_to_comment raises RuntimeError
session.assistant:
Good — `FakeGitHubIssues` accepts `comments_with_urls` which maps issue numbers to `IssueComment` objects (which have `.id` and `.body`). That's how `get_comment_by_id` finds comments. The test will need to seed a comment with the `objective_comment_id` value.
The plan is solid. Let me finalize it.

Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Objective Roadmap Table Sync Validation Current context: (wt:erk-slot-04) (br:plnd/O8832-rename-github-to-loc-03-06-1802) (pr:#8888)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/O883"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 4eb4862b-92c2-4b59-a519-0b92ca4623f7 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/serialized-noodling-engelbart.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.
